annotations.label: Generalized Riemann hypothesis (GRH)

anchor.snippet: The Generalized Riemann hypothesis asserts that all nontrivial zeros of Dirichlet L -function

annotations.note: Mathlib has `RiemannHypothesis` but no analogous Prop for Dirichlet L-functions.

annotations.label: Dirichlet character

anchor.snippet: A Dirichlet character

annotations.note: `DirichletCharacter R n` is defined as `MulChar (ZMod n) R`.

annotations.label: Dirichlet L-function

anchor.snippet: we define the corresponding Dirichlet L -function

annotations.note: Defined as the (analytically continued) L-series of a Dirichlet character.

annotations.label: Convergence and analytic continuation of Dirichlet L-function

anchor.snippet: For every complex number s such that Re s > 1 this series is absolutely convergent

annotations.note: Convergence for `re s > 1` is `LSeriesSummable_of_one_lt_re`; the analytic continuation is encoded by `DirichletCharacter.LFunction` together with `differentiable_LFunction`.

annotations.label: Imprimitive and primitive Dirichlet character

anchor.snippet: We say that the Dirichlet character

annotations.note: `IsPrimitive χ` is defined as `conductor χ = n`; imprimitivity is the negation.

annotations.label: Trivial zeros of Dirichlet L-function

anchor.snippet: the L-function satisfies a certain functional equation which allows us to define trivial zeros

annotations.note: The functional equation that determines the trivial zeros is formalized, but the notion of trivial zero is not packaged as a definition.

annotations.label: Nontrivial zeros of Dirichlet L-function

anchor.snippet: Any other zeros are called nontrivial zeros

annotations.note: Mathlib has no predicate distinguishing trivial vs. nontrivial zeros of a Dirichlet L-function.

annotations.label: Trivial character yields ordinary Riemann hypothesis

anchor.snippet: Taking the trivial character

annotations.note: The identification of the trivial-character L-function with `riemannZeta` is formalized, but the corresponding GRH→RH implication is not stated.

annotations.label: Effective Dirichlet's theorem on arithmetic progressions

anchor.snippet: More effective version of Dirichlet's theorem on arithmetic progressions

annotations.note: Only the qualitative Dirichlet theorem (`Nat.infinite_setOf_prime_and_eq_mod`) is in Mathlib; no effective bound on the least prime under GRH.

annotations.label: Subgroup of multiplicative group has small generators

anchor.snippet: Every proper subgroup of the multiplicative group

annotations.note: Bach's GRH-conditional bound on small generators of proper subgroups of (ℤ/nℤ)* is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Miller-Rabin test runs in polynomial time

anchor.snippet: G. Miller showed that the Miller-Rabin test is guaranteed to run in polynomial time

annotations.note: No Miller-Rabin primality test or its GRH-conditional polynomial-time analysis is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: AKS primality test runs in polynomial time

anchor.snippet: Manindra Agrawal, Neeraj Kayal and Nitin Saxena proved unconditionally that the AKS primality test

annotations.note: The AKS primality test is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shanks–Tonelli algorithm runs in polynomial time

anchor.snippet: The Shanks–Tonelli algorithm is guaranteed to run in polynomial time

annotations.note: The Shanks–Tonelli algorithm and its GRH-conditional runtime are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ivanyos–Karpinski–Saxena algorithm runs in polynomial time

anchor.snippet: The Ivanyos–Karpinski–Saxena deterministic algorithm

annotations.note: The Ivanyos–Karpinski–Saxena factoring algorithm is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Small primitive root modulo p

anchor.snippet: For every prime p there exists a primitive root mod p

annotations.note: Existence of primitive roots is in Mathlib, but the GRH-conditional polylog bound on their size is not.

annotations.label: Improved Pólya–Vinogradov estimate

anchor.snippet: Estimate of the character sum in the Pólya–Vinogradov inequality can be improved

annotations.note: No Pólya–Vinogradov inequality (unconditional or GRH-improved) is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Grönwall: imaginary quadratic fields with class number 1

anchor.snippet: Grönwall showed that the generalized Riemann hypothesis implies that Gauss's list of imaginary quadratic fields

annotations.note: The conditional finiteness/listing of imaginary quadratic fields with class number 1 is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hardy–Littlewood: Chebyshev's conjecture

anchor.snippet: Hardy and Littlewood showed that the generalized Riemann hypothesis implies a conjecture of Chebyshev

annotations.note: Chebyshev's bias conjecture and its GRH-conditional proof are absent from Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hardy–Littlewood: weak Goldbach conjecture

anchor.snippet: Hardy and Littlewood showed that the generalized Riemann hypothesis implies Goldbach weak conjecture

annotations.note: Neither the weak Goldbach conjecture nor its GRH-conditional version is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chowla: first prime in arithmetic progression

anchor.snippet: Chowla showed that the generalized Riemann hypothesis implies that the first prime in the arithmetic progression

annotations.note: Chowla's GRH-conditional bound on the least prime in an arithmetic progression is not formalized.

annotations.label: Hooley: Artin's conjecture on primitive roots

anchor.snippet: Hooley showed that the generalized Riemann hypothesis implies Artin's conjecture on primitive roots

annotations.note: Artin's primitive root conjecture is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weinberger: Euler's idoneal numbers list complete

anchor.snippet: Weinberger showed that the generalized Riemann hypothesis implies that Euler's list of idoneal numbers is complete

annotations.note: Euler's idoneal numbers and Weinberger's GRH-conditional theorem are not formalized.

annotations.label: Ono–Soundararajan: Ramanujan's quadratic form

anchor.snippet: Ono & Soundararajan (1997) showed that the generalized Riemann hypothesis implies that Ramanujan's integral quadratic form

annotations.note: Ramanujan's ternary form representation result conditional on GRH is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Dunn–Radziwill: Patterson's conjecture on cubic Gauss sums

anchor.snippet: Alexander (Alex) Dunn and Maksym Radziwill proved Patterson's conjecture on cubic Gauss sums

annotations.note: Patterson's conjecture on cubic Gauss sums (and Dunn–Radziwill's conditional proof) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Dedekind zeta-function

anchor.snippet: The Dedekind zeta-function of K is then defined

annotations.note: `NumberField.dedekindZeta K s` is the Dedekind zeta-function of a number field K.

annotations.label: Extended Riemann hypothesis (ERH)

anchor.snippet: The extended Riemann hypothesis asserts that for every number field K each nontrivial zero

annotations.note: No ERH Prop on `NumberField.dedekindZeta` is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ordinary RH follows from ERH for the rationals

anchor.snippet: The ordinary Riemann hypothesis follows from the extended one

annotations.note: No ERH⇒RH implication is stated, since ERH itself is not defined.

annotations.label: GRH equivalent to ERH for abelian extensions

anchor.snippet: Generalized Riemann hypothesis for Dirichlet L-functions is equivalent to ERH

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks both GRH and ERH as Props, so this equivalence is not formalized.

annotations.label: ERH equivalent to RH for Artin L-functions

anchor.snippet: ERH is equivalent to Riemann Hypothesis for Artin L-functions

annotations.note: Artin L-functions are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Effective Chebotarev density theorem under ERH

anchor.snippet: The ERH implies an effective version

annotations.note: The Chebotarev density theorem (effective or otherwise) is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Weinberger: number fields with class number 1 under ERH

anchor.snippet: Weinberger (1973) showed that ERH implies that any number field with class number 1

annotations.note: Weinberger's ERH-conditional class-number-1 theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Odlyzko: sharper estimates for discriminants and class numbers

anchor.snippet: Odlyzko (1990) discussed how the ERH can be used to give sharper estimates

annotations.note: Odlyzko's ERH-conditional discriminant/class-number bounds are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Selberg class

anchor.section: Generalized Riemann hypothesis for Selberg class
anchor.snippet: We say that Dirichlet series

annotations.note: The Selberg class of L-functions is not defined in Mathlib (`Mathlib.NumberTheory.SelbergSieve` concerns the unrelated Selberg sieve).

annotations.label: Generalized Riemann hypothesis for Selberg class

anchor.section: Generalized Riemann hypothesis for Selberg class
anchor.snippet: Generalized Riemann hypothesis for Selberg class states that all nontrivial zeros

annotations.note: Without the Selberg class, GRH for it is not defined either.

annotations.label: RH for Selberg class implies extended Riemann hypothesis

anchor.snippet: Artin L -functions and Dedekind zeta functions belong to Selberg class

annotations.note: Mathlib has neither Selberg-class membership of Dedekind/Artin L-functions nor the implication.

annotations.label: Ramanujan L-function and the Dedekind eta function

anchor.snippet: One example can be Ramanujan L -function

annotations.note: No Ramanujan tau L-function is defined in Mathlib; the Δ/η modular forms exist but not the associated L-series.
